rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that all of plaintiffs' claims against it fail even if defendant owed no duty to Elaine Percy. Duty is not an element of strict products liability. (Elsheref v. Applied Materials, Inc. (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 451.) Defendant moved solely for summary judgment and did not include an alternate request for summary adjudication of individual causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15a.m., not 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD L. BASS et al VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Manwill's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Bass was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's argument regarding exposure aboard the Exxon Houston is moot. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs' claim for premises owner/contractor liability fails even if defendant did not retain control or have knowledge of a preexisting concealed hazardous condition. Defendant's objections to Mr. Manwill's declaration are overruled. The Court treated this as solely a motion for summary judgment as defendant failed to bring a proper motion for summary adjudication. (CRC 3.1350(b), (d).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15a.m., not 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD KOEPKE et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Hertz Corporations Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2014 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant The Hertz Corporation's motion for reconsideration of the Court's order denying its motion for summary judgment and motion for summary adjudication issues 1, 4, 5, 6, 7 and 8 is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate new or different facts, circumstances or law to warrant reconsideration. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15a.m., not 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
